description: Regulated pollutants, EPA NESHAP emission limits, and monitoring requirements for Portland cement manufacturing facilities.

regulatory_framework.primary_regulation: 40 CFR 63 Subpart LLL - National Emission Standards for Hazardous Air Pollutants from the Portland Cement Manufacturing Industry
regulatory_framework.applicability: All Portland cement plants with kilns (major and area sources)
also_applicable: 40 CFR 60 Subpart F - NSPS for Portland Cement Plants (PM from kilns)
40 CFR 60 Subpart Y - NSPS for Coal Preparation Plants
State Implementation Plans (SIPs) for criteria pollutants
Title V operating permits (40 CFR Part 70)

particulate_matter_PM.description: Filterable PM from kiln/clinker cooler and other point sources
particulate_matter_PM.neshap_limit_kiln: 0.02 lb/ton clinker (or 2.0 mg/dscm for existing sources after 2013 amendments)
particulate_matter_PM.monitoring: CEMS (PM CEMS or opacity) or periodic stack testing (Method 5/5I)
particulate_matter_PM.control_technology: Baghouse (fabric filter) is standard. ESP less common for new installations.

sulfur_dioxide_SO2.description: From sulfur in raw materials and fuel
sulfur_dioxide_SO2.neshap_limit: 0.4 lb/ton clinker (or operating limit based on performance test)
sulfur_dioxide_SO2.control: Primarily controlled by raw material selection. Spray dryer absorber or dry sorbent injection if needed.

nitrogen_oxides_NOx.description: Thermal NOx from high flame temperature and fuel NOx from nitrogen in fuel
nitrogen_oxides_NOx.neshap_limit: 1.5 lb/ton clinker (existing sources) or state-specific limits

carbon_monoxide_CO.neshap_limit: No explicit NESHAP limit but used as operating parameter for D/F control
carbon_monoxide_CO.monitoring: CO CEMS typically required as operating parameter
carbon_monoxide_CO.notes: CO excursions may trigger MACT bypass requirements for D/F compliance

hydrogen_chloride_HCl.description: From chloride in raw materials and fuels (especially alternative fuels)
hydrogen_chloride_HCl.control: Alkaline raw meal naturally absorbs most HCl. Dry sorbent injection for additional control.

mercury_Hg.description: Trace mercury in raw materials (limestone, clay) and some fuels
mercury_Hg.monitoring: Mercury CEMS or sorbent trap monitoring (Method 30B)

total_hydrocarbons_THC.description: Organic compounds from raw material organic content and incomplete combustion
total_hydrocarbons_THC.neshap_limit: 24 ppmvd corrected to 7% O2 (or site-specific operating limit)
total_hydrocarbons_THC.control: Process optimization, raw material selection, organic content management

dioxins_furans_DF.description: PCDD/PCDF formed during combustion and in cooler temperature windows (200-400C)
dioxins_furans_DF.neshap_limit: 0.2 ng TEQ/dscm corrected to 7% O2 (or temperature/CO operating limits)
dioxins_furans_DF.monitoring: Stack testing (Method 23) or compliance via operating parameter limits (temperature, CO)
control: Rapid quench of gases through 200-400C window

stack_testing.frequency: As required by permit, typically every 2.5 or 5 years for performance tests

compliance_determination.description: How CEMS data translates to compliance status. Critical distinction: a single data point above the limit is NOT the same as a compliance violation.

averaging_periods.compliance_basis: 30-day rolling average of valid 6-minute CEMS data blocks
averaging_periods.how_calculated: Average of all valid 6-minute averages within a rolling 30-day operating window. Non-operating hours and substitute data periods are handled per 40 CFR 75.
averaging_periods.single_block_exceedance: A single 6-minute block above the limit does NOT constitute a compliance violation. It is one data point in the rolling average calculation.
averaging_periods.when_violation_occurs: When the 30-day rolling average exceeds 0.02 lb/ton clinker (kiln) or 0.07 lb/ton clinker (cooler).
averaging_periods.reporting_trigger: Document any 6-minute block exceeding 3x the limit as an operational anomaly. Report when the 30-day rolling average exceeds the limit.
averaging_periods.common_confusion: Operators frequently see a single high reading and panic. Three consecutive 6-minute blocks above the limit during startup does NOT mean you are in violation — check the 30-day rolling average.

averaging_periods.how_calculated: Same as PM — rolling average of valid 6-minute CEMS data blocks over 30 operating days.
averaging_periods.single_block_exceedance: A single high SO2 reading is not a violation. Check the rolling average.
averaging_periods.when_violation_occurs: When the 30-day rolling average exceeds 0.4 lb/ton clinker.
averaging_periods.reporting_trigger: Report when rolling average exceeds limit.

averaging_periods.how_calculated: Rolling average of valid 6-minute CEMS data blocks over 30 operating days.
averaging_periods.single_block_exceedance: Single high readings are not violations.
averaging_periods.when_violation_occurs: When the 30-day rolling average exceeds 1.5 lb/ton clinker (existing sources).
averaging_periods.reporting_trigger: Report when rolling average exceeds limit.

averaging_periods.compliance_basis: 30-day rolling average (if CEMS) or stack test
averaging_periods.how_calculated: If HCl CEMS installed: rolling average of 6-minute data. If no CEMS: compliance demonstrated by periodic stack test.
averaging_periods.when_violation_occurs: When 30-day rolling average exceeds 3 ppmvd @ 7% O2, or when stack test result exceeds the limit.
averaging_periods.reporting_trigger: Report when rolling average or test result exceeds limit.

averaging_periods.compliance_basis: 30-day rolling average (sorbent trap) or stack test
averaging_periods.how_calculated: Sorbent trap method provides integrated samples. Rolling average calculated from trap results. Stack test (Method 30B) provides point-in-time compliance.
averaging_periods.when_violation_occurs: When 30-day rolling average exceeds 21 lb/million tons clinker (existing sources).
averaging_periods.reporting_trigger: Mercury exceedance requires notification to EPA Region within 24 hours in addition to standard reporting.
averaging_periods.special_note: Mercury has the most stringent notification requirements due to HAP status.

averaging_periods.how_calculated: Rolling average of valid 6-minute CEMS data blocks.
averaging_periods.when_violation_occurs: When 30-day rolling average exceeds 24 ppmvd @ 7% O2.
averaging_periods.reporting_trigger: Report when rolling average exceeds limit.

averaging_periods.compliance_basis: Stack test only — no continuous monitoring
averaging_periods.how_calculated: Compliance demonstrated by periodic stack test using Method 23. No rolling average applies.
averaging_periods.when_violation_occurs: When stack test result exceeds 0.2 ng TEQ/dscm @ 7% O2. Alternatively, compliance via operating parameter limits (temperature, CO).
averaging_periods.reporting_trigger: Report stack test failure. Retest within 60 days.

averaging_periods.compliance_basis: 6-minute average — any single exceedance is reportable
averaging_periods.how_calculated: UNLIKE all other pollutants, opacity uses 6-minute averages directly for compliance. There is no 30-day rolling average.
averaging_periods.when_violation_occurs: When ANY single 6-minute average exceeds 20% opacity.
averaging_periods.reporting_trigger: Document and report immediately per permit requirements.
averaging_periods.critical_distinction: Opacity is the EXCEPTION to the rolling average rule. A single 6-minute reading above 20% IS a deviation.

key_distinctions: 6-minute CEMS data block above the limit ≠ compliance violation (except opacity)
30-day rolling average above the limit = compliance violation
Opacity is the exception: 6-minute averages are used directly for compliance
Substitute data rules apply when CEMS is down (40 CFR 75)
Missing data exceeding 10% of operating hours in a quarter = potential violation itself
During startup: CEMS data is still collected and counts toward rolling averages unless facility has approved alternative plan
